title: The AI That Teaches You

modules.title: Meet Khanmigo: More Than a Chatbot
modules.sub: First look at an AI tutor built for students like you
modules.description: You jump straight in and have a real conversation with Khanmigo, testing how it responds to questions you'd actually ask in class. You compare your first impressions against what you already expect from a teacher, noting what surprises you about how the AI behaves.

modules.title: How Does It Know What You Need?
modules.sub: Uncovering the logic behind personalized AI learning
modules.description: You investigate how AI tutors adapt to individual learners by experimenting with different types of questions and skill levels. You make decisions about what data the AI might be using and discuss whether that personalization feels helpful, creepy, or somewhere in between.

modules.title: When Your AI Tutor Gets It Wrong
modules.sub: Catching mistakes and deciding when not to trust AI
modules.description: You deliberately try to trip up an AI tutor and record every error, gap, or misleading answer you find. Using your findings, you build an early version of a trust checklist that flags the kinds of mistakes learners should watch out for.

modules.title: Comparing Three AI Learning Tools
modules.sub: Side-by-side audit of real AI tools made for learners
modules.description: You test three different AI learning tools on the same task and score each one using a shared set of criteria. You weigh up the trade-offs between tools, deciding which strengths and weaknesses matter most to a student who needs accurate, honest help.

modules.title: Can AI Replace Your Favorite Teacher?
modules.sub: Exploring what human teachers do that AI simply cannot
modules.description: You interview or reflect on what makes your best teacher effective, then map those qualities against what AI tutors can and cannot replicate. You write a short verdict explaining one capability and one risk that sets AI tutors apart from human teachers.

modules.title: Design a Better AI Tutor Yourself
modules.sub: Creating your own rubric and reimagining smarter AI tutoring
modules.description: You design a detailed rubric for evaluating any AI tutor, drawing on everything you tested and discovered across the course. You then sketch out a concept for an improved AI tutor that addresses the biggest gaps you identified, and present it as a pitch to your peers.

synopsis: You're a student tester hired to audit AI tutors â including Khanmigo and others â by actually using them, breaking them, and judging whether they help or mislead learners like you. Each module puts you in a scenario where you must decide how much to trust the AI's guidance and why.
rationale: As AI tutors enter classrooms globally, students in the 11-13 band are using them without any framework for assessing their accuracy, limitations, or effect on real learning.
learning_outcome: Students can critically assess an AI tutoring tool using a self-designed rubric and explain one specific way AI tutors differ from human teachers in both capability and risk.
source_signals: Meet Khanmigo: the student tutor AI being tested in school districts | 60 Minutes
